refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation end-to-end communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly state the latency performance metric and its impact on safety, clarifying the measurable requirement and its safety-critical nature.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency during teleoperation sessions under defined network conditions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms; conduct safety validation scenarios to confirm safe remote operation.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable and consistent network link quality to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking data exchange within latency constraints.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize communication protocol stack for low latency (e.g., UDP-based, prioritized packets).
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes to reduce network hops and processing delays.
DP3: Use dedicated network link with QoS guarantees for teleoperation data.
DP4: Design telemetry and tasking data formats for minimal payload size.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP3: Use dedicated network link with QoS guarantees for teleoperation data.

potential_sources: Coupling between network link and telemetry/tasking data flows
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP isolates latency control to the network link with QoS, minimizing cross-domain coupling and enabling modular implementation.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of network infrastructure supporting QoS.
Risk of network congestion or failure impacting latency.
Assumes telemetry and tasking data can be prioritized appropriately.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating edge computing (DP2) if network QoS alone is insufficient to meet latency targets.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Responsible for managing teleoperation communication, including network link management, telemetry, and tasking data handling to meet latency requirements.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data with QoS guarantees.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives vehicle telemetry data for operator feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends operator commands to vehicle control systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused on teleoperation specifics; higher diffusion risk.
Vehicle module handles physical control but not communication management; poor containment.
Best containment for teleoperation latency DP; low diffusion risk.
Safety services monitor alerts but do not manage teleoperation communication.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP to Teleoperation module centralizes latency management, minimizing diffusion and enabling clear ownership.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle module for telemetry and tasking data.
Dependency on Network Link interface performance and availability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and monitoring between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules to mitigate interface complexity risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (dedicated network link with QoS) directly addresses the latency FR with measurable performance criteria and manageable assumptions.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as Teleoperation module encapsulates latency management.
Interface dependencies with Vehicle and Network Link are well defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows highest containment score and clear ownership, supporting modular design.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified end-to-end via Teleoperation module test harness and network monitoring tools.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine interface definitions between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
Plan for fallback or redundancy in network link to mitigate latency risks.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignments satisfy the latency FR with clear DP and IM mapping, manageable risks, and verification feasibility. No further decomposition needed at this stage.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable and consistent network link quality to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking data exchange within latency constraints.

revise.why: Current FR, DP, and IM are consistent and well-aligned with safety-critical latency requirement.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports QoS for teleoperation data.
Telemetry and tasking data can be prioritized effectively.
open_risks: Network congestion or failure impacting latency.
Interface complexity between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
evidence_needed: Latency performance measurements under operational network conditions.
Interface compliance tests between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
